name: MindCare Hub API
description: REST API for small mental-health practices: patient onboarding, specialist scheduling, confidential medical records, billing, and notifications for psychologists, psychiatrists, and therapists.

problemStatement.problemTitle: Fragmented tools for small therapy practices
problemStatement.problemDescription: Independent psychologists, psychiatrists, and therapists often juggle spreadsheets, paper notes, separate calendars, and ad-hoc billing. That creates scheduling conflicts, weak access control on sensitive clinical data, and no single view of a patient's care journey.
problemList: Double bookings and unclear specialist availability across the week
Medical notes stored without role-based confidentiality rules
Patients, staff, and specialists using different systems for appointments and invoices
Manual follow-up for activation emails, reminders, and payment status
No unified API for a future patient portal or clinic admin dashboard

solution.solutionTitle: One API built for mental-health workflows

solutionList.title: Role-based access for the whole clinic
solutionList.description: JWT authentication with user types (patient, specialist, staff, admin) and service-layer checks on appointments, records, and payments.

solutionList.title: Scheduling that respects clinic rules
solutionList.description: Appointments enforce clinic hours (09:00–19:00), advance booking windows, overlap detection, and specialist daily caps.

solutionList.title: Confidential medical records
solutionList.description: Records support standard, sensitive, and highly_sensitive levels; patients cannot read highly sensitive entries; specialists own their documentation.

solutionList.title: Billing aligned with real practices
solutionList.description: Bills, cash and bank-transfer payments, Stripe online payments, refunds, and insurance claims in one billing module.

solutionList.title: Production-ready container deploy
solutionList.description: Multi-stage Docker image, Gunicorn, health checks, and external PostgreSQL plus Redis for cache, rate limits, and Celery.

metricsList: 6 Django domain apps (users, specialists, appointments, medical, billing, notification)
REST API v2 under /api/v2/ (appointments router mounted at /appointments/)
129+ Python modules in apps/ excluding migrations and tests
Docker image Python 3.11 slim, Gunicorn 3 workers × 2 threads

metrics.label: Django apps
metrics.description: users, specialists, appointments, medical, billing, notification

metrics.label: Auth
metrics.description: SimpleJWT with refresh rotation and blacklist on logout

layers.name: Presentation (clients)
layers.description: Web and mobile clients used by patients, therapists, and clinic staff.
responsibilities: Collect user input and display API responses

layers.name: API gateway & edge
layers.description: TLS termination and routing to the Django application on EC2.

layers.name: Application layer
layers.description: Django 5 + DRF monolith in Docker with domain apps and service classes.
responsibilities: Business rules in services, not views

layers.name: Data & cache
layers.description: Persistent storage and Redis for cache, limits, and task broker.
responsibilities: Transactional data for clinical and billing entities

layers.name: Async & integrations
responsibilities: Send notifications and process pending deliveries

designPatterns.title: Service layer
designPatterns.description: Business logic in *Service classes (AppointmentService, MedicalRecordService, UserService) keeps views thin and testable.

designPatterns.title: Repository (Django ORM)
designPatterns.description: Models and custom managers encapsulate queries; services compose ORM access with transactions.

designPatterns.title: Decorator — cross-cutting
designPatterns.description: @api_error_handler and @rate_limit wrap views for consistent errors and throttling.

designPatterns.title: Strategy — payments
designPatterns.description: Cash, bank transfer, and Stripe online flows share billing service abstractions with different processors.

designPatterns.title: Factory — notifications
designPatterns.description: Notification factories build channel-specific messages from templates.

designPatterns.title: Observer — signals & tasks
designPatterns.description: Django signals and Celery tasks react to domain events (e.g. pending notifications beat task).

scalabilityStrategies.title: Stateless API containers
scalabilityStrategies.description: Run multiple Gunicorn workers per EC2 instance; scale horizontally with additional EC2 instances behind a load balancer.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Managed PostgreSQL
scalabilityStrategies.description: RDS handles backups, storage growth, and read-replica option for reporting without blocking writes.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Redis for hot paths
scalabilityStrategies.description: Offload rate-limit counters and cache to Upstash/Redis to reduce DB load on public specialist listings.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Async notification delivery
scalabilityStrategies.description: Celery queues prevent SMTP/SMS latency from blocking HTTP request threads.

securityStrategies.title: JWT + role permissions
securityStrategies.description: IsPatient, IsSpecialist, IsAdminOrStaff combined with per-view get_permissions() and service-layer filters.

securityStrategies.title: Medical confidentiality tiers
securityStrategies.description: standard / sensitive / highly_sensitive with explicit deny rules for patients and staff.

securityStrategies.title: Production TLS & cookies
securityStrategies.description: SECURE_SSL_REDIRECT, HSTS, secure session and CSRF cookies in config.settings.production.

securityStrategies.title: Audit middleware
securityStrategies.description: AuditLoggingMiddleware logs sensitive operations; filter reduces PII in log output.

securityStrategies.title: Rate limit profiles
securityStrategies.description: SENSITIVE on login, PUBLIC on specialist browse, WRITE_OPERATION on mutations.

cacheStrategies.name: Default Django cache
cacheStrategies.description: django-redis backend pointing to Redis DB 1 with KEY_PREFIX mind_care_hub
cacheStrategies.coverage: Rate limits, optional view caching, token storage patterns

cacheStrategies.name: Activation & reset tokens
cacheStrategies.description: Short-lived keys in cache via apps/core/shared.py helpers

cacheStrategies.name: Celery broker
cacheStrategies.description: Separate Redis database index 0 for task queue (base settings)

architectureFeatures.title: Unified API envelope
architectureFeatures.description: APIResponse wraps status, message, data, pagination, and metadata for every client.

architectureFeatures.title: OpenAPI-first docs
architectureFeatures.description: Spectacular generates schema from DRF viewsets for Swagger and ReDoc.

architectureFeatures.title: Health & app info
architectureFeatures.description: Public /api/v2/health/ and /api/v2/app-documentation/ for probes and versioning.

architectureFeatures.title: Domain-driven apps
architectureFeatures.description: Each bounded context (users, specialists, appointments, medical, billing) is a Django app with its own services and serializers.

requestFlow.title: Client request
requestFlow.description: Patient or specialist calls REST endpoint with JWT in Authorization header (except public routes).

requestFlow.title: Gateway & DRF
requestFlow.description: Nginx forwards to Gunicorn; DRF authentication, permissions, and @rate_limit execute.

requestFlow.title: Service layer
requestFlow.description: View delegates to domain service (e.g. AppointmentService.create) with validation and transactions.

requestFlow.title: Persistence
requestFlow.description: ORM writes to PostgreSQL; cache updated for tokens or rate counters in Redis.

requestFlow.title: Response envelope
requestFlow.description: APIResponse returns JSON { status, message, data } to the client.

eventFlow.title: Domain event
eventFlow.description: Appointment booked, bill issued, or user registered triggers signal or explicit task call.

eventFlow.title: Celery task
eventFlow.description: Notification task queued on Redis broker for email/SMS rendering.

eventFlow.title: Template render
eventFlow.description: NotificationTemplate + user preferences determine channel and content.

eventFlow.title: Delivery
eventFlow.description: SMTP or Twilio sends message; failures logged to SystemLog / Sentry.

techDecisions.title: Django monolith vs microservices
techDecisions.problem: Small therapy group needs fast delivery and simple ops, not distributed complexity.
techDecisions.solution: Single Django codebase with clear app boundaries and service classes; one Docker image on EC2.
techDecisions.outcome: Lower operational overhead; scale vertically and with extra EC2 instances first.

techDecisions.title: PostgreSQL on RDS
techDecisions.problem: Relational data with strict integrity for appointments, records, and billing.
techDecisions.solution: Managed RDS PostgreSQL with connection pooling (CONN_MAX_AGE 600).
techDecisions.outcome: ACID transactions for clinical and financial data; dev uses SQLite in development.py.

techDecisions.title: SimpleJWT
techDecisions.problem: SPA/mobile clients need stateless auth for patients and specialists.
techDecisions.solution: JWT access + refresh with rotation; custom claims for user_type.
techDecisions.outcome: Works well for API-first clients; requires blacklist app for secure logout.

techDecisions.title: External Redis (Upstash)
techDecisions.problem: Rate limits and cache should survive API container restarts on EC2.
techDecisions.solution: django-redis to cloud Redis; compose file does not bundle Redis container.
techDecisions.outcome: Shared cache across multiple API instances behind ALB.

codeExamples.title: Medical record confidentiality & access control
codeExamples.description: MedicalRecordService enforces who can read clinical notes—critical for therapist/patient trust and staff boundaries.

files.name: api_response.py

features.title: JWT authentication & role-based access
features.description: Email login with SimpleJWT access/refresh tokens, custom claims (user_type, full_name), and DRF permissions for patient, specialist, staff, and admin flows.
highlights: Account activation and password reset via cache tokens

features.title: Specialist profiles & availability
features.description: Public discovery of psychologists, psychiatrists, and therapists with services, categories, and date-based available slots for patient booking.
highlights: Staff/admin manage specialists and clinic services

features.title: Appointment booking rules
features.description: Service-layer validation for clinic hours, booking windows, overlaps, and role-scoped querysets for patients and specialists.
highlights: Cancel, reschedule, today, and stats actions
Virtual session and room fields on Appointment model

features.title: Confidential medical records
features.description: Clinical notes tied to appointments with standard, sensitive, and highly_sensitive levels; patients blocked from highly_sensitive; 24h specialist edit window.
highlights: Export and audit-log endpoints for compliance workflows

features.title: Billing, invoices & Stripe
features.description: Bills from appointments, cash and bank-transfer payments, Stripe PaymentIntents, refunds, insurance claims, and PDF invoices.
highlights: my-bills and overdue listings for patients

features.title: Redis-backed rate limiting
features.description: Profile-based sliding window limits (SENSITIVE, PUBLIC, WRITE_OPERATION, etc.) using Django cache / Redis.

features.title: Multi-channel notifications
features.description: Email and SMS templates with Celery tasks for appointment reminders, activation, and billing—notification app present; HTTP admin API not mounted in urls.
highlights: Twilio and SMTP configuration via environment

features.title: OpenAPI & standardized responses
features.description: drf-spectacular schema, Swagger UI, ReDoc, and uniform APIResponse envelope for success and errors.

features.title: Docker production image
features.description: Multi-stage Python 3.11 image, non-root django user, entrypoint migrations, and Gunicorn WSGI server for EC2 or ECS deploy.
highlights: Waits for cloud PostgreSQL and Redis

cloudServices.name: Amazon EC2
cloudServices.purpose: Runs Docker Compose web service (mindcare_api) for the Django API behind security group allowing 8001/443

cloudServices.name: Amazon RDS (PostgreSQL)
cloudServices.purpose: Managed primary database for users, appointments, medical records, and billing (DB_HOST in .env)

cloudServices.name: Upstash Redis
cloudServices.purpose: Cache (DB 1), rate limiting, activation tokens, and Celery broker URL (REDIS_HOST / REDIS_URL)

cloudServices.name: Stripe
cloudServices.purpose: Online card payments, PaymentIntents, and webhooks for billing reconciliation

cloudServices.name: SMTP (e.g. Gmail / SES)
cloudServices.purpose: Account activation, password reset, invoice and reminder emails

cloudServices.name: Twilio
cloudServices.purpose: SMS notifications for appointments and system alerts

cloudServices.name: Sentry (optional)
cloudServices.purpose: Error tracking when SENTRY_DSN is set in production

dockerFiles.description: Single-service compose file; PostgreSQL and Redis are external cloud instances, not containerized.
dockerFiles.content:
services:
  web:
    build:
      context: ..
      dockerfile: docker/dockerfile
    container_name: mindcare_api
    env_file: ../.env
    environment:
      DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: config.settings.production
      ENVIRONMENT: production
    ports:
      - "8001:8000"
    volumes:
      - logs_volume:/app/logs
    restart: unless-stopped
# Cloud: RDS PostgreSQL + Upstash Redis

dockerFiles.description: Python 3.11 slim bookworm; builder installs requirements; runtime runs as non-root django user.
dockerFiles.content:
FROM python:3.11-slim-bookworm AS builder
# gcc, libpq-dev, pip install -r requirements.txt
FROM python:3.11-slim-bookworm AS runtime
USER django
EXPOSE 8000
ENTRYPOINT ["/bin/sh", "/app/scripts/entrypoint.sh"]
# Gunicorn: 3 workers, 2 threads, migrate on start

dockerFiles.description: Waits for DB_HOST/REDIS_HOST, runs migrate, optional superuser/dummy data, starts Gunicorn.
dockerFiles.content:
# nc -z DB_HOST DB_PORT  (PostgreSQL ready)
# nc -z REDIS_HOST REDIS_PORT  (Redis ready)
python manage.py migrate --noinput
gunicorn config.wsgi:application --bind 0.0.0.0:${PORT:-8000} --workers 3 --threads 2

httpEndpoints.description: Email/password login for patients, specialists, staff, and admins. Returns user profile and access/refresh tokens.
Creates a patient user; email activation flow uses cache-backed tokens.
Requires Bearer access token and refresh_token in body. Ensure token_blacklist app is installed for production.
Returns profile for the authenticated user (any role).
Browse active mental-health professionals (psychologists, psychiatrists, therapists) for booking.
Returns bookable time slots for a specialist on a given date (YYYY-MM-DD).
Authenticated list scoped by role: patients see own bookings, specialists see their schedule, staff/admin see broader sets. Note: mounted without /api/v2/ prefix.
Creates appointment after clinic hours, overlap, and advance-booking validation in AppointmentService.
Specialist or staff cancels a booking; enforces business rules on status transitions.
Returns records the caller may access per confidentiality and role (MedicalRecordService).
Specialist documents session notes linked to an appointment; validates diagnosis length and confidentiality.

example.diagnosis: Patient reports improved sleep; continue CBT exercises.

httpEndpoints.description: Patients and authorized roles view invoices tied to completed appointments.
